## Summary

Adds `/admin/forensics/[jobId]` — a SIPOC Forensic View that traces every pipeline stage for a given evaluation job, answering the six-question standard (what enters, what leaves, who owns, what validates, what repairs, what prevents dirt downstream).

Key addition: `inferred_pass` result type — differentiates log-proven stage completion from artifact-evidence inference. Artifact exists ≠ stage fully passed; the UI shows ⊛ INFERRED PASS (emerald badge) vs ✓ PASS (green badge).

**API** — `GET /api/admin/forensics/[jobId]` reads from `evaluation_jobs`, `evaluation_artifacts`, `pipeline_logs`. No schema changes.

**Stage inference for failed jobs:**
```
artifact evidence → inferred_pass (stage produced output)
phase highwater → inferred_pass (stage before failed phase)
log/timeline events → pass (proven by audit trail)
```

## Scope

- `app/admin/forensics/[jobId]/page.tsx` — new forensic view page (admin-only)
- `app/api/admin/forensics/[jobId]/route.ts` — new API endpoint (read-only)
- `app/admin/pipeline-health/page.tsx` — added forensic link per job
- `app/admin/jobs/dead-letter/page.tsx` — added forensic link per job
- `__tests__/forensicStageInference.test.ts` — 18 unit tests for inference logic
- `docs/SIPOC_EVALUATION_PROCESS.md` — added Forensic Diagnostics section

## Visual Evidence

Admin-only page using the high-contrast admin standard (slate-900/800 text, white tables, red-800 error badges). Failed stages auto-expand on page load. Tested on real production jobs (successful: 0fea6d6c, failed: 2446968a).

## Accessibility

Standard HTML table with semantic `<thead>`/`<tbody>`. Clickable rows use `cursor-pointer` with visible hover state. Color badges have ring borders for non-color differentiation. All text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios on white/slate backgrounds.

## Browser Targets

Standard HTML table + Tailwind utilities. No browser-specific APIs. Works in all modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

## Risks & Anomalies

- `inferred_pass` is directionally correct but artifact existence does not guarantee downstream validation passed. UI labels make this distinction visible to admins.
- No schema changes — reads existing tables only.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

None. API reads from existing Supabase tables (`evaluation_jobs`, `evaluation_artifacts`, `pipeline_logs`) via authenticated admin-only endpoint. No external data sources.

## Internal Process Leakage

None. Page is admin-only (behind `requireAdmin` guard). Error codes, artifact types, and pipeline internals are appropriate for admin visibility. User-facing `last_error` is the sanitized message already shown to authors.

## Input → Action → Output

- **Input**: Job ID from URL param
- **Action**: Fetch job + artifacts + logs, infer stage results, assemble forensic trace
- **Output**: JSON response consumed by admin-only React page displaying stage-by-stage progression

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

No public-facing metrics exposed. All data is admin-only. The forensic view displays internal pipeline state (stage results, error codes, artifact types) which is appropriate for admin diagnostics but never exposed to authors.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None. This is a read-only diagnostic view. Does not modify the evaluation pipeline, add stages, or change processing behavior. No new background jobs or queues.

## Latency Impact

None. Read-only API endpoint with 3 simple Supabase queries (jobs, artifacts, logs). No pipeline latency impact — this is an admin diagnostic tool, not part of the evaluation path.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: true — admin-only diagnostic view, no evaluation pipeline logic affected.
